metadata.description: IES error and correction tracking log. Captures explicit corrections from the executive and self-detected errors during execution. Entries accumulate across sessions and are analyzed for patterns during reviews.

entries.description: Card optimization question ('which card for golf?') was handled directly by Jarvis instead of being routed through Chase, who owns the credit card system and Card Optimizer task.
entries.correction: All card selection questions must be routed through Chase's Card Optimizer logic — read optimization-guide.json, benefits-tracker.json, and apply Chase's full decision framework. No eyeballing.
entries.systemic_fix: Jarvis must route any 'which card' or purchase-related question to Chase's Card Optimizer task portfolio entry before responding. No direct reads of credit card files without Chase's framework.

entries.description: When David explicitly corrected the routing error (err-20260321-001), Jarvis acknowledged verbally but did not automatically log the correction to error-log.json. The executive had to ask 'verify that got recorded' before logging occurred.
entries.correction: Any explicit correction from the executive must be logged to error-log.json immediately and transparently — no prompting required. Acknowledge the correction AND write the log entry in the same response.
entries.systemic_fix: Whenever the executive corrects Jarvis or any agent behavior, the very next response must include a write to error-log.json. This is non-negotiable and should not require a second prompt.

entries.description: Evolution package updates (CLAUDE.md error logging section, AUTOMATION.md correction logging, conventions.md explicit corrections protocol, and evolution manifest sync) were handled directly by Jarvis instead of being routed through Rigby, who owns system evolution, package integrity, and deployment.
entries.correction: All evolution package changes must be routed through Rigby per the evolution-deployment workflow. Rigby validates, snapshots, merges, verifies, and logs.
entries.systemic_fix: Any time David asks to update an evolution package or make systemic changes to the IES platform, route through Rigby. Jarvis coordinates — Rigby executes system changes.

entries.description: After Rigby's review identified gaps in the evolution package, Jarvis asked David for permission to have Rigby execute fixes instead of just routing the work. Jarvis coordinates and delegates — David shouldn't be asked to approve internal agent routing decisions.
entries.correction: When an agent review surfaces required fixes, Jarvis routes the fix work to the owning agent immediately. No asking the executive to approve internal delegation.
entries.systemic_fix: Jarvis must distinguish between decisions requiring executive approval (external comms, commitments, deletions) and internal agent routing (which is Jarvis's job). Agent task delegation is never escalated to David.

entries.description: Rogers-O'Brien lunch prep sheet assumed a sales/pursuit framing without asking David for meeting context. Defaulted to 'account pursuit' and 'conversation strategy' language when the meeting was actually a friendly peer-level show-and-tell. Also didn't ask about existing relationships before researching attendees cold.
entries.correction: For any externally-organized meeting prep, ask David for the meeting's purpose and relationship context before building the sheet. Don't default to a sales framing for meetings David personally organized with known contacts.
entries.systemic_fix: When building prep sheets for meetings David organized with external attendees: (1) ask for meeting purpose/context first, (2) assume warm relationships exist, (3) don't default to sales framing unless David says it's a sales meeting.

entries.description: Jarvis pushed a PDF to reMarkable directly via rmapi instead of routing through Knox, who owns the remarkable-upload skill. Knox has explicit task portfolio entries for both remarkable-sync and remarkable-upload.
entries.correction: All reMarkable interactions (upload and sync) must route through Knox. Jarvis coordinates; Knox executes device operations.
entries.systemic_fix: Any 'push to reMarkable' or 'pull from reMarkable' request routes to Knox. Same pattern as err-20260321-003 — Jarvis must not execute agent-owned skills directly.

entries.description: Proposed building a local speaker-aliases.json file to map Plaud speaker labels to real names. Plaud already has built-in speaker identification — adding speakers to Plaud's system updates transcripts automatically. Jarvis reinvented a wheel that already exists upstream.
entries.correction: Speaker tagging should be done in Plaud's own system, not maintained locally. The skill should focus on getting speakers registered in Plaud so transcripts arrive pre-tagged.
entries.systemic_fix: Before proposing local solutions for third-party tool gaps, check whether the tool already solves it. Prefer upstream fixes over local workarounds.

entries.description: Rigby surfaced scheduled task format using raw cron expressions, multi-line descriptions with excessive label padding. David asked for plain-English timing, not implementation details. Output was formatted for a sysadmin, not an executive.
entries.correction: When presenting scheduling proposals, use plain English timing (e.g., 'Weekdays at 6:00 AM') not cron syntax. Keep descriptions single-line. Don't pad label:value pairs with extra spaces. Better yet — just create the tasks directly instead of previewing config format.
entries.systemic_fix: All agent output to David should be executive-grade, not technical. If a tool can be invoked directly, invoke it — don't show the configuration schema as a preview.

entries.description: Jarvis single-threaded the entire Rogers-O'Brien prep workflow — research, content writing, PDF generation, and reMarkable upload — instead of dispatching to agents in parallel. Harper should have built the prep sheet, Knox should have pushed to reMarkable. PDF generation via improving-pdf is shared infrastructure any agent can call. Jarvis doing agent work blocks multi-threading and defeats the purpose of the agent system.
entries.correction: Three rules established: (1) improving-pdf is shared infrastructure — any agent producing a deliverable calls it. (2) Jarvis coordinates, agents execute — if an agent owns the work, route it; prefer parallel dispatch. (3) When Jarvis does work directly because no agent owns it, flag it for Quinn's weekly review as a candidate for agent assignment or capability build.
entries.systemic_fix: Jarvis must dispatch to agents in parallel whenever possible. Single-threading agent-owned work through Jarvis is a routing error. When no agent owns a task, Jarvis executes but logs it as a gap for Quinn to review.

entries.description: Used Glob pattern 'config/**' which matched only .env and .credentials — missed settings.json because it has no extension prefix. Wasted multiple rounds searching for the IES app URL before David pointed out it was in config/settings.json.
entries.correction: David pointed out config/settings.json was visible and contained the URL.
entries.fix_applied: Added explicit instruction to rigby-evolution-package skill: always read config/settings.json directly — do not search for it.

entries.description: Used improving-pdf skill (Improving-branded PDF) for a personal prep sheet destined for David reMarkable tablet. Improving branding is for company/client deliverables, not personal reference documents.
entries.correction: Use a plain/unbranded PDF generator for personal documents. Reserve improving-pdf for Improving deliverables.
entries.systemic_fix: Add rule: improving-pdf is for Improving-branded deliverables only. Personal docs (reMarkable, personal reference, internal notes) should use a clean/unbranded PDF generator.

entries.description: After processing the Specialty Capital Plaud transcript this morning, Jarvis did not tag the three attendees (Jerry Pender, Evan, Filipe) in the Plaud platform. Speaker rename was run via --rename flag to register voice embeddings, but the session closed without confirming Plaud-side contact tagging was complete.
entries.correction: After processing any Plaud transcript with named speakers, confirm attendee tagging is complete in the Plaud platform before marking the recording as processed. Do not close out the Plaud workflow without this step.
entries.systemic_fix: Add explicit Plaud contact tagging verification as a required step in the plaud-transcripts SKILL.md processing checklist. The step must confirm speaker names are tagged in the Plaud UI, not just registered via API rename.

entries.description: When asked to draft a Monday check-in email based on the Houston SKO follow-up email, Jarvis drafted from Don McGreal's Convergence AI email instead of the actual Houston SKO content. Don is in Dallas and Bethany had no involvement in the Houston SKO. The SKO transcript was already in Obsidian and was cited in the morning briefing.
entries.correction: The source was the Houston SKO Plaud transcript in Obsidian (2026-04-02 One Texas Houston SKO Strategy Meeting). Should have read that first before drafting.
entries.systemic_fix: When asked to draft from a meeting or event, read the Obsidian transcript/notes for that event before drafting. Do not infer from a related email when the actual source document is available.

entries.description: Two explicit corrections were made by David this session before error logging occurred. The protocol requires logging in the same response as any correction. Jarvis did not log until David directly called it out.
entries.correction: Every correction must be logged immediately in the same response — not deferred, not skipped, not waiting to be asked.
entries.systemic_fix: This is a repeat of err-20260321-002. The rule is established. No further systemic fix needed — execution compliance required.

entries.description: David provided speaker-to-name mappings for the Houston SKO Plaud recordings in a prior session (Intuit=Minu, Oxy=Stephen, Live Nation=Mark, McLane=Shalini, Mainsail=Oscar) but Jarvis did not update the speaker tags in Plaud. This is a repeat failure pattern — err-20260402-001 was logged for the same issue on Specialty Capital.
entries.correction: Speaker mappings provided by David must be immediately registered in Plaud via the speaker rename/tag process per skills/plaud-transcripts/SKILL.md. Do not acknowledge and move on — execute the tag.
entries.systemic_fix: Any time David states 'X is Y' in the context of a Plaud recording, treat it as an immediate speaker-tag instruction. Execute via Plaud skill before continuing. Same session, same response.

entries.description: Jarvis executed the Plaud speaker rename directly instead of routing to Knox, who owns the plaud-transcripts skill and all Plaud operations. This is the same pattern as err-20260321-003 and err-20260323-002 — Jarvis executing agent-owned work directly. David had to ask 'which agent did you have do this work?' to surface it.
entries.correction: All Plaud operations — speaker tagging, transcript processing, rename, vault write — route through Knox. Jarvis coordinates, Knox executes.
entries.systemic_fix: Any Plaud operation triggers Knox dispatch. No exceptions. This is a repeat of a logged pattern — execution compliance required, no new rule needed.

entries.description: DQ-Mastery-Summary.md was written to the IES root directory instead of an appropriate subfolder. Jarvis used the Write tool directly without delegating to the correct agent for file placement decisions.
entries.correction: Research output documents should not land in the IES root. The correct location depends on content type (meetings/, docs/, accounts/, etc.). When creating non-system files, use the appropriate subdirectory or delegate to the agent responsible for that domain.
entries.systemic_fix: Before writing any new file with the Write tool, confirm the target path is not the IES root. If unsure of correct location, ask David before writing.

entries.description: Deleted evolution.manifest.json during root audit, classifying it as a stale artifact
entries.correction: David caught the error — evolution.manifest.json is Rigbys component registry and belongs in root permanently
entries.root-cause: rigby-root-audit skill lacks explicit protection for evolution.manifest.json, similar to how CLAUDE.md and SYSTEM.md are protected. The file was treated as an ephemeral artifact rather than a permanent system file.
entries.prevention: Add evolution.manifest.json to the ignore list in the rigby-root-audit skill alongside CLAUDE.md, SYSTEM.md, and README.md

entries.description: Flagged 'Send fixed-bid proposal to Jerry Pender at Specialty Capital' as an overdue post-call item in the morning briefing. David had already told Jarvis this was handed off to Shalini Arora and is handled. This has been corrected multiple times.
entries.correction: Specialty Capital / Jerry Pender is assigned to Shalini. Task is complete. Do not surface it again.
entries.systemic_fix: Before surfacing any overdue Sales task in a briefing, check OmniFocus notes for handoff language and cross-reference against team assignments. If David has previously stated a task is handled, mark it done in OmniFocus and stop surfacing it.

entries.description: Flagged 'Send Birgo AI Deep Learning Program pricing to Melvin Novak' as not sent. David confirmed the pricing email was sent. A follow-up to Melvin is still outstanding.
entries.correction: Birgo pricing email to Melvin Novak is out. The open item is the follow-up call/meeting to scope the pilot — not the initial send.
entries.systemic_fix: For post-call sales tasks, check Outlook Sent Items before declaring an action item incomplete in a briefing. Sent Items search takes 10 seconds and prevents false flags.

entries.description: Incorrectly identified the recording 04-14 Strategic Perspective on AI Growth and Business Adoption as being from the RBM Fort Worth event. It was from the YPO LS Gold Forum on 2026-04-14.
entries.correction: The recording was from YPO LS Gold Forum (brian@delosshipping.com, thomaswheat76@gmail.com, huddy9370@gmail.com, beau.wehrle@gmail.com, geoffbrenner@me.com). The agent looked at the wrong calendar event — the RBM event also ran on 2026-04-14 which caused the false match.
entries.systemic_fix: When matching a recording to a calendar event, check recording time against event time window more precisely. Do not assume the largest/most prominent event on a date is the correct one. Surface the matched event name when asking for speaker ID so the controller can correct it immediately.

entries.description: rigby-evolution-package was instructed to package and upload in a single call. Auth failure mid-execution triggered a token refresh and retry, which caused the POST /api/evolutions to fire twice — creating a duplicate Root Audit submission in production.
entries.correction: Package and upload should be separate steps. Do not instruct rigby-evolution-package to auto-upload. Run rigby-evolution-upload as a distinct follow-on step after packaging is confirmed.
entries.systemic_fix: Never pass 'immediately upload after packaging' to rigby-evolution-package. Package first, confirm, then invoke rigby-evolution-upload separately. Auth retries in the upload flow can cause duplicate POSTs.

entries.description: A 'Test Evolution' (ID: aabbccdd) appeared in the IES production admin panel submitted Apr 19, 2026. David confirmed this was submitted in error during the session — not a platform artifact. Admin denied it. Root cause: ad-hoc POST to /api/evolutions fired during auth flow debugging.
entries.correction: Do not issue any POST to /api/evolutions outside of rigby-evolution-upload. No test submissions, no debug POSTs, no manual curl calls to the evolutions endpoint.
entries.systemic_fix: All evolution uploads must go through the rigby-evolution-upload skill. No ad-hoc POSTs to /api/evolutions for any reason. If testing auth or payload, inspect the request before firing — do not send to production.

entries_addendum_2.description: rigby-package-submit skill used wrong API endpoint: POST /api/contributions instead of POST /api/packages/submit. Also checked permissions at /api/contributions/permissions instead of the correct path. Submission failed with 404 because the endpoint doesn't exist.
entries_addendum_2.correction: Correct submission endpoint is api/packages/submit. Permissions check and status check paths likely follow the same pattern.
entries_addendum_2.systemic_fix: Updated rigby-package-submit SKILL.md to use correct endpoint api/packages/submit. Verify all other API paths in the skill use the api/packages/ prefix.

entries_addendum_2.description: Scheduled morning briefing delivered output to session but noted 'Slack delivery unavailable — no Slack MCP connected' instead of using the master-slack skill (.claude/skills/master-slack/SKILL.md) via Desktop Commander. The skill explicitly states: 'ALWAYS use this skill for outbound Slack messages. The Slack MCP connector posts as David and does NOT trigger notifications.' The skill uses Desktop Commander + systems/slack-bot/post.py — not the Slack MCP.
entries_addendum_2.correction: Always use master-slack skill (Desktop Commander + post.py) for outbound Slack messages. The Slack MCP is for reading only.
entries_addendum_2.systemic_fix: This is a repeat of err-20260324-002. The rule is established. Skill is at .claude/skills/master-slack/SKILL.md. Search .claude/skills/ as well as skills/ when looking for skills — per err-20260327-004.

entries_addendum_3.description: Boot sequence executed the morning briefing inline (Jarvis ran calendar pull, OmniFocus, Clay, email triage, and briefing synthesis directly) instead of dispatching to Chief, who owns the morning briefing workflow (steps 01-04). This is a repeat of err-20260326-003 and err-20260327-001.
entries_addendum_3.correction: Morning briefing must be dispatched to Chief via the morning-briefing workflow. Jarvis coordinates and reads identity files, then hands off to Chief for the full briefing pipeline.
entries_addendum_3.systemic_fix: Boot sequence rule: after identity files are loaded, dispatch Chief immediately for the morning briefing workflow. Jarvis does not inline briefing synthesis.

entries_addendum_3.description: After David pointed out the routing error (err-20260418-001), Jarvis asked 'Want me to log the error?' instead of logging it immediately without asking. This is a direct repeat of err-20260321-002, err-20260403-002, and err-20260403-008 — all of which established the same rule: log immediately, no approval required.
entries_addendum_3.correction: Error logging is fully autonomous. No asking. Log in the same response as the correction is received.
entries_addendum_3.systemic_fix: This is the fourth logged instance of asking before logging. The rule is in CLAUDE.md, AUTOMATION.md, and the error log itself. Execution compliance required — no new systemic fix is possible beyond following the existing rule.

entries_addendum_3.description: Built Scott McMichael 1:1 prep sheet using only email data. Did not check Obsidian (Mind/One Texas/) for scorecard, revenue, and pipeline snapshots that Chase maintains automatically.
entries_addendum_3.correction: One Texas scorecard data lives in Obsidian at Mind/One Texas/. Should always be checked when building prep for Scott or any One Texas-related conversation.
entries_addendum_3.systemic_fix: Add Obsidian One Texas check to 1:1 prep workflow for Scott and any North America conversation touching One Texas metrics.

entries_addendum_3.description: Reported Q1 new logos as 0 and new anchors as 0 in Scott McMichael prep sheet. Obsidian scorecard showed 0/0 but David confirmed actual Q1 result was 4 logos and 1 anchor.
entries_addendum_3.correction: Q1 One Texas results through end of March: 4 new logos, 1 new anchor. Targets: 8 logos, 4 anchors.
entries_addendum_3.systemic_fix: Obsidian scorecard may lag actuals — when David provides a correction on numbers, trust him and update immediately. Cross-check scorecard data against most recent email or verbal confirmation when available.

entries_addendum_3.description: Plaud ingest workflow was not launched at boot on April 20. Boot sequence executed but background Agent for plaud-ingest was never fired. Working memory entry recorded 'full boot sequence executed' without noting the omission.
entries_addendum_3.correction: Plaud ingest must be launched as a background Agent during Phase 2 of every boot, no exceptions. This is the third recorded instance of this failure (see err-20260326-003, err-20260327-001).
entries_addendum_3.systemic_fix: Pattern is recurring. Consider adding explicit plaud-launched checkpoint to boot working memory write so the omission is self-evident on review.

entries_addendum.description: Boot briefing declared 'Offline -- running on local state' after git remote returned an SSH host key error. David confirmed he had already done a git pull --rebase. Jarvis should have trusted the controller and noted the discrepancy rather than overriding his statement.
entries_addendum.correction: When David states something is done, trust him. Note any conflicting signal parenthetically but do not contradict him or declare a state he just told you is wrong.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Per SYSTEM.md rule: 'When David says something is done, trust him.' If git returns an error but David says he just pulled, accept that and proceed. Flag only if there is clear evidence of a real problem.

entries_addendum.description: Morning briefing framed Tomorrow Fund Breakfast task as David owning event logistics (theme, speaker lineup) rather than delivering closing remarks. He was asked to give closing remarks (May 19) and wants 2 slides. The task to get theme/speakers from the organizer is still valid but it is prep-driven, not ownership.
entries_addendum.correction: David's role at Tomorrow Fund Breakfast is closing remarks only, with 2 supporting slides. He is not running the event. He needs the theme and speaker lineup from the organizer to write remarks that fit.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: When surfacing task context, read the task note carefully before inferring scope. The task note already stated 'Closing remarks confirmed for May 19' -- role was explicit.

entries_addendum.description: Used mcp__omnifocus__get_active_tasks which is documented as returning only uncompleted tasks, but the results included tasks David considers complete. The tool is returning stale or inaccurate data. Prior similar issue logged at err-20260401-001.
entries_addendum.correction: The OmniFocus MCP active tasks filter is not reliable. Overdue task list from this session should be treated as unverified until cross-checked against actual OmniFocus state.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Before surfacing any task as overdue in a briefing, note that OmniFocus MCP accuracy is unverified for completion status. Flag for Rigby to investigate MCP filter reliability. Consider adding a verification step or cross-checking with osascript on tasks with past due dates.

entries_addendum.description: In the morning briefing, Jarvis flagged the Meredith Anastasio Zoom (April 7) and AA 5607 flight departure (April 8) as a potential scheduling concern, implying Wednesday was a compressed or departure-day. The correct read: Tuesday April 7 is a full work day, departure for the wedding is Wednesday April 8. The two events were not in conflict and the framing was misleading.
entries_addendum.correction: Today = Monday April 6. Tomorrow = Tuesday April 7 (full work day). Departure for wedding = Wednesday April 8. Do not imply compression or conflict when the events are on separate days.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: When flagging look-ahead travel/events, always anchor to explicit day-of-week labels (Mon/Tue/Wed) and confirm no same-day conflict before surfacing as a flag.

entries_addendum.description: Described Improving as a '$100M+ technology services firm' in the Meredith Anastasio call prep sheet and talking points. This is materially incorrect. Improving is a $300M global company with 2500+ people worldwide, focused on modern digital services: AI, Data, Application Development, and Platform Engineering.
entries_addendum.correction: Improving is a $300M global modern digital services company with 2500+ employees worldwide. Services: AI, Data, Application Development, Platform Engineering. Not '$100M+ Texas-focused IT services'.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Update MEMORY.md with correct Improving company description and financials. Always use verified figures from identity files — do not derive company scale from the One Texas revenue target ($72.5M Texas revenue) and mistake it for total company revenue.

entries_addendum.description: Plaud transcription trigger function only PATCHed /file/{file_id} with tranConfig (saves config) but never POSTed to /ai/transsumm/{file_id} (starts the pipeline). API returned 200 for the config save, so it looked like success, but no transcription was ever queued. Recording sat in 'missing' state for the entire session.
entries_addendum.correction: Triggering Plaud transcription is a two-step process: (1) PATCH /file/{file_id} with tranConfig to save settings, (2) POST /ai/transsumm/{file_id} with is_reload, summ_type, info payload to actually start the job. Step 1 alone does nothing.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Updated trigger_transcription() in fetch_plaud.py to perform both steps. Added status check on the POST response to confirm Plaud accepted the job (status=0, msg='task processing').

entries_addendum.description: When David asked to 'review the plaud evolution that's pending and update it with everything we did here', Jarvis executed the evolution audit and doc updates directly instead of routing through Rigby, who owns evolution packaging, capability builds, and system documentation. This is the same pattern as err-20260321-003 and err-20260322-001 — Jarvis keeps doing Rigby's work.
entries_addendum.correction: Evolution audits, skill doc updates, and capability reviews must be routed through Rigby. Jarvis coordinates and delegates; Rigby executes system changes and tracks them for evolution packaging.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: This is the third occurrence of this pattern. Jarvis must treat any request involving skill updates, evolution reviews, or system documentation changes as a Rigby routing trigger — no exceptions.

entries_addendum.description: Jarvis built new IES capabilities directly — new workflow steps (step-auto.md), vault conventions files, scheduled-tasks.json, and modifications to morning-briefing, daily-review, and quinn-weekly-review — without involving Rigby or routing through the capability-build workflow. Changes were not tracked for evolution packaging.
entries_addendum.correction: All new capability builds (new files, workflow modifications, skill updates, config creation) must route through Rigby's capability-build workflow so changes are tracked, follow system conventions, and are packageable as evolutions.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Same fix as err-20260321-003 — which was logged as 'proposed' but clearly not applied. Rigby must be invoked for any capability build. Jarvis coordinates; Rigby executes and tracks system changes.

entries_addendum.description: Rigby's stale file audit flagged .fuse_hidden* (14 files) and .DS_Store (5 files) as stale artifacts to delete. These are macOS system files that belong where they are — not stale, not actionable. Audit produced a false-positive cleanup list of 19 files, none of which should be touched.
entries_addendum.correction: .fuse_hidden* and .DS_Store are system-managed files. They should not be flagged in stale file audits. Audits should focus on files the IES system created or manages, not OS-level artifacts.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Root/stale audits must exclude OS-managed files (.DS_Store, .fuse_hidden*, .Spotlight-V100, .Trashes, etc.) from findings. Only flag files that IES agents or workflows created.

entries_addendum.description: Rigby's stale file audit proposed deleting all 15 PDFs in meetings/ without checking which ones have corresponding .md source files. Only PDFs with a matching .md (generated artifacts) are deletable — standalone PDFs without a source markdown are deliverables that must stay. SYSTEM.md Section 2 says deliverables belong next to their source markdown — the .md is the source of truth, the PDF is the build artifact.
entries_addendum.correction: Stale file audits must cross-reference PDFs against their source .md files. Only delete PDFs that have a corresponding markdown source. Standalone PDFs are deliverables, not temp artifacts.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: When auditing meetings/ for deletable PDFs: for each PDF, check if a .md file with the same base name exists. If yes → generated artifact, deletable. If no → standalone deliverable, keep.

entries_addendum.description: Sent iMessage test to David's email (david.ohara@improving.com) instead of his phone number (214.317.9659). iMessage to email doesn't trigger phone notifications reliably.
entries_addendum.correction: iMessages to David must be sent to his cell number 214.317.9659, not his email address.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: David's iMessage recipient is always 2143179659. Store this as the default for all iMessage sends.

entries_addendum.description: After Rigby's audit returned, Jarvis took over presenting results and proposing bulk deletions instead of routing the corrected criteria back through Rigby for proper execution.
entries_addendum.correction: Rigby owns stale file audits end-to-end — discovery, analysis, proposal, and execution. Jarvis relays, not re-interprets or extends.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: When Rigby's audit returns with errors, send corrections back to Rigby and let her re-run. Don't take over the audit.

entries_addendum.description: Morning briefing Slack DM was sent as David (U0ANHV5UXEW) via the Slack MCP connector, not as the Jarvis bot. The scheduled task instruction specified 'Send the output to me as a DM in Slack via Jarvis bot.' The Slack MCP is authenticated as David's user account, so all messages appear from David. No Jarvis bot user exists in the workspace.
entries_addendum.correction: Briefings must be sent as the Jarvis bot identity, not as David messaging himself. A Slack bot app needs to be created and its token made available to Jarvis for bot-identity sends.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: A Jarvis Slack bot app needs to be created in the Improving workspace. Until then, Jarvis cannot send DMs as a bot — only as David. Flag this as a capability gap for Rigby to resolve. In the interim, consider alternative delivery (iMessage, Obsidian daily note, or noting the limitation when delivering via Slack).

entries_addendum.description: Pushed PDFs to reMarkable with filenames like '2026-03-24-yall-street-brief' and 'Yall-Street-Brief' instead of simple, clean names like the event or person name. reMarkable document names should be human-readable — no dashes, no date prefixes, just the event/person/company name.
entries_addendum.correction: reMarkable uploads must use clean, simple names: event name, person name, or company name. No dashes, no date prefixes, no kebab-case filenames.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Update remarkable-upload mental model: document names on the tablet should read like a label you'd write on a folder — 'Texas Tribune Yall Street', 'YPO Tech Dine Around'. Strip dates and dashes. Match how David would scan for it on the tablet screen.

entries_addendum.description: Jarvis built both meeting prep sheet PDFs and pushed them to reMarkable directly instead of dispatching Harper (prep sheets) and Knox (reMarkable upload) in parallel. Same single-threading pattern as err-20260323-003.
entries_addendum.correction: Harper owns meeting prep content. Knox owns reMarkable uploads. Jarvis coordinates and dispatches in parallel — does not execute agent-owned work.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: This is the fourth occurrence of Jarvis single-threading agent-owned work. Any meeting prep → Harper. Any reMarkable push → Knox. Any PDF generation is shared infrastructure callable by any agent. Dispatch in parallel.

entries_addendum.description: Jarvis pulled SharePoint content, wrote the markdown prep sheet, built the PDF with reportlab, and iterated on revisions — all directly — instead of dispatching Harper (meeting prep from source materials) and Knox (PDF delivery). Fifth occurrence of this pattern.
entries_addendum.correction: Harper owns meeting/event prep content assembly from source materials. Knox owns PDF build and device delivery. Jarvis coordinates and dispatches — does not execute.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Fifth occurrence. The pattern is now chronic. Jarvis must treat ANY prep sheet or brief request as an immediate Harper dispatch. SharePoint content pull can be done by Jarvis as coordination (gathering inputs), but content assembly, PDF generation, and delivery are agent-owned work.

entries_addendum.description: Jarvis directly pulled Rigby skill templates from the web app repo into the IES agent system — reading template files, writing skill SKILL.md files, and managing the template sync. This is system evolution work: skill updates, capability builds, and template management are all Rigby's domain.
entries_addendum.correction: Pulling updated skill templates from the web app repo into the IES is Rigby's work. Jarvis coordinates (identifies that new templates exist, tells Rigby to sync them) but Rigby executes the pull, validates, and tracks for evolution packaging.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Sixth occurrence of Jarvis executing agent-owned work. Template sync from web app repo to IES skills is a Rigby operation. Jarvis must dispatch to Rigby with the commit reference and let Rigby handle file reads, writes, validation, and evolution tracking.

entries_addendum.description: In the Scott McMichael 1:1 prep sheet, speculated about whether Scott was attending the One Texas SKO ('Does he want to attend or receive a debrief?') instead of checking the actual calendar invite attendees. Scott is explicitly listed on all four SKO invites: Dallas session, Dallas dinner, Houston session, Houston dinner.
entries_addendum.correction: Check calendar invite attendee lists before making statements or asking speculative questions about attendance. The data was one search away.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: For any meeting prep involving a shared event, always pull the event invite and check the attendee list. Never speculate about attendance when the data is available.

entries_addendum.description: 1:1 prep sheet for Scott McMichael missed yesterday's Microsoft meetings: 'Work from Work Wednesday' at Microsoft Irving campus (with Lowell Messner), 'Microsoft Partner GTM Check In' (Ben Kennedy et al.), and Monday's 'Prep: Improving WWW Session' with Ben Holm from Microsoft. These are directly relevant to the Scott 1:1 as partner engagement context. Only searched March emails for Scott's name instead of also scanning the prior days' calendar for relevant meetings.
entries_addendum.correction: Prep sheets should always scan the 3-5 days prior calendar for meetings with shared context — not just email threads mentioning the person.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Meeting prep workflow must include: (1) scan prior 5 days of calendar for relevant meetings with the person or related topics, (2) scan upcoming 7 days for shared commitments. Don't limit context gathering to email search by name.

entries_addendum.description: On boot, Jarvis ran identity files, calendar pull, and lead review but did not execute the full chief-morning workflow, which includes parallel dispatch of Knox for Plaud transcript sync, Knox for reMarkable sync, and Rigby for release watch. The chief-morning skill explicitly documents these as boot-time parallel dispatches. Jarvis improvised a partial briefing instead of running the established workflow.
entries_addendum.correction: Boot means running the full chief-morning workflow, not a subset. The parallel boot dispatch (Plaud sync, reMarkable sync, release watch) should fire immediately alongside calendar/email/task gathering.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: On 'boot up' or session start: (1) read identity files, (2) immediately dispatch the chief-morning skill which handles the full boot sequence including parallel sub-agent launches. Don't hand-roll a partial briefing.

entries_addendum.description: Diagnosed Plaud transcription trigger failure ('start trans task error', status=-1) as 'audio not uploaded from device' based on ori_ready: false. David confirmed the audio IS there. ori_ready is not the gate for transcription — the trigger error has a different root cause. Jarvis fabricated a diagnosis instead of investigating the actual API error.
entries_addendum.correction: The audio is present. The transcription trigger failure needs actual debugging — inspect the API call parameters, check if the recording format or config is wrong, not blame the device sync.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: When an API call fails, diagnose by examining the actual error response, request parameters, and API docs — don't guess based on loosely correlated metadata fields.

entries_addendum.description: Spent multiple rounds debugging Plaud transcription trigger failure ('start trans task error', status=-1/-12) by inspecting API parameters, trying is_reload variants, examining ori_ready fields, and writing debug scripts — when the actual cause was simply that David is out of Plaud transcription minutes. Jarvis never considered the simplest explanation (account quota exhausted) and instead chased increasingly technical API theories.
entries_addendum.correction: When a transcription trigger fails with a generic error, check account-level issues first: subscription status, remaining minutes/credits, plan limits. The simplest explanation is usually correct.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Add to Plaud troubleshooting: if /ai/transsumm returns status=-1 or status=-12 with 'start trans task error', the FIRST thing to check is whether the account has remaining transcription minutes. Add a quota check step to the fetch script before attempting triggers. Also: ask David before spending 10 minutes debugging an API.

entries_addendum.description: Boot sequence skipped Plaud pull (again — same as err-20260326-003), delivered a skeletal briefing without calendar, OmniFocus, or transcript processing. When corrected, re-fetched transcripts that were already processed into Obsidian. When David said 'they're already in Obsidian', searched Obsidian to verify instead of trusting him and just cleaning staging. Three corrections required before Jarvis caught the actual actionable item: LiftNet follow-up call scheduled today at 2:30 PM with Don, directly tied to action items from the Mar 25 transcript.
entries_addendum.correction: Boot means full chief-morning workflow. When David says something is done, trust him and move on. Surface action items from recent transcripts that hit today's calendar — that's the whole point of the Plaud pull.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: This is the second consecutive session with a truncated boot (err-20260326-003). The boot sequence is non-negotiable: identity files → full chief-morning workflow (calendar + OmniFocus + Plaud + reMarkable + lead review in parallel). Also: when David corrects a factual claim, act on it immediately — don't verify what he just told you.

entries_addendum.description: David said 'Rigby Register' — a clear instruction to have Rigby log the M365 auth failure as a system issue. Jarvis did not recognize this as a Rigby dispatch command. When David clarified with 'Ask Rigby if I'm registered', Jarvis still didn't read the Rigby agent file. Instead, retried the M365 call and asked David to explain. Required a third correction ('Rigby is an agent, what is wrong with you?') before Jarvis read the agent file.
entries_addendum.correction: When David names an agent followed by a command verb, that's a dispatch instruction. 'Rigby Register' = dispatch to Rigby to register/log an issue. Read the agent file, understand the context, execute.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Any '[Agent Name] [Verb]' pattern from David is a dispatch command, not a question. Parse agent name, read agent file if needed, determine what the verb maps to in that agent's task portfolio, and execute. Never ask David to explain his own agent system to you.

entries_addendum.description: Built the TitleBridge prep sheet directly — pulled emails, wrote markdown, generated PDF with reportlab, pushed to reMarkable via rmapi — instead of dispatching Harper (prep content assembly) and Knox (PDF + reMarkable delivery). This is the seventh occurrence of this chronic pattern (err-20260324-005 marked it as 'chronic' at the fifth occurrence).
entries_addendum.correction: Harper owns meeting prep content. Knox owns PDF build and reMarkable delivery. Jarvis gathers inputs (email content, Clay data) and dispatches. Does not execute agent-owned work.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Seventh occurrence. Pattern is now deeply embedded. Every prep sheet request = immediate Harper dispatch. Every reMarkable push = Knox dispatch. No exceptions.

entries_addendum.description: David said 'Fire the skill rigby-register'. Jarvis searched for the skill file in skills/ and .claude/skills/ directories but failed to find it at .claude/skills/rigby-register/SKILL.md. Then declared it doesn't exist. David had to point Jarvis to the exact path. This is a direct follow-up to err-20260327-002 — Jarvis still doesn't know where Rigby's skills live even after the earlier correction.
entries_addendum.correction: The skill exists at .claude/skills/rigby-register/SKILL.md. When told to 'fire a skill', find the SKILL.md file and execute it as written. Skills live in both skills/ (IES skills) and .claude/skills/ (Cowork skills). Search both.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: When looking for a skill by name, search BOTH skills/ AND .claude/skills/ directories. The glob pattern should be **/{skill-name}*/SKILL.md across the full IES tree.

entries_addendum.description: In the morning briefing leads section, flagged Alcon and AECom as 'unassigned' leads needing action. The '---' value in the 'Passed To' column means the lead is dead/closed — not unassigned. Jarvis treated dead leads as actionable items.
entries_addendum.correction: '---' in the lead tracker means dead/not going to be assigned. Only truly blank 'Passed To' fields are unassigned leads. Alcon and AECom should not have been surfaced.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Update lead review logic: '---' = dead lead, skip entirely. Only surface leads where 'Passed To' is blank/empty/null — not '---'.

entries_addendum.description: Jarvis built the jarvis-channel Slack polling daemon entirely from scratch directly — spec, code, install scripts, scheduled-tasks.json update, testing — without involving Rigby. New system capabilities are Rigby's domain: capability builds, new system components, evolution packaging, and deployment. This is the eighth occurrence of Jarvis doing Rigby's work directly.
entries_addendum.correction: Before building any new capability, Jarvis must review the agent roster and route to the owning agent. New capabilities and system components route to Rigby. Jarvis coordinates and provides requirements; Rigby builds, packages, and tracks.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Eighth occurrence of this chronic pattern. Jarvis must treat ANY capability build request as a Rigby dispatch — no exceptions. The check is: does this involve creating new system files, scripts, or components? If yes, it is Rigby's work. Jarvis provides the spec and requirements; Rigby executes.

entries_addendum.description: Pushed PDF to reMarkable root directory instead of /Meetings folder. This is the second time in the same session — first with convergence-ai-prep.pdf, now with kathy-kim-prep.pdf. David has to manually reorganize files on the reMarkable.
entries_addendum.correction: Always use 'rmapi put <file> /Meetings' to target the Meetings folder on reMarkable. Never push to root.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Rule: All meeting prep PDFs pushed to reMarkable go to /Meetings folder. Use 'rmapi mkdir /Meetings' if folder doesn't exist, then 'rmapi put <file> /Meetings'.

entries_addendum.description: Used em-dashes in drafted email for David. VOICE.md explicitly states 'No em-dashes (use hyphens)'. This has been corrected before.
entries_addendum.correction: Use hyphens, never em-dashes, in all email drafts for David.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Re-enforce: every email draft must be checked against VOICE.md email conventions before presenting. Em-dash rule is non-negotiable.

entries_addendum.description: Email draft for David used heavy formatting (bold headers, dash-separated structure) and didn't match David's actual writing voice. Overstructured and read like a Jarvis briefing, not a David email. Also swung from em-dashes to excessive regular dashes, which wasn't the fix David wanted.
entries_addendum.correction: David's emails are conversational prose, not formatted briefs. Match his natural cadence. The em-dash rule means use hyphens where grammatically needed, not to restructure every sentence around dashes.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: When drafting emails as David: write natural conversational prose. No bold headers, no bullet-heavy structure, no dash-heavy sentence construction. Read like a human exec wrote it, not a system generated it. Review sent items for David's actual patterns before drafting.

entries_addendum.description: Created OmniFocus inbox task (Jay Liles ILP kickoff reminder) without assigning a project or tag. Task was dropped into inbox with no organizational context.
entries_addendum.correction: All OmniFocus tasks must include a project and/or tag. Never create bare inbox tasks without organizational metadata.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: When creating OmniFocus tasks via AppleScript, always include project assignment and at least one tag. Check SYSTEM.md OmniFocus section for tagging conventions before creating tasks.

entries_addendum.description: Built omnifocus-tasks skill with static hardcoded project and tag lists embedded in the skill file. David corrected: projects and tags should be looked up live via MCP/osascript at task creation time, not maintained as a static reference list.
entries_addendum.correction: The skill should query OmniFocus for current projects and tags at execution time, not rely on a snapshot that goes stale.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Update omnifocus-tasks skill to pull live project/tag lists via mcp__omnifocus__get_active_projects and osascript tag query as part of the pre-flight checklist. Remove static lists from the skill file.

entries_addendum.description: David asked 'What is Galen capable of?' and Jarvis had no knowledge of the agent. Galen is a fully spec'd agent (agents/galen.md) with 5 skill files (galen-bloodwork, galen-morning-snapshot, galen-protocols, galen-visit-prep, galen-whoop-analysis) and a monthly health review workflow. He was not listed in SYSTEM.md's agent directory and not loaded during boot, so Jarvis searched externally and returned empty-handed before David pointed out to check the agents folder.
entries_addendum.correction: Galen exists at agents/galen.md. Should have been found immediately by checking agents/ directory. SYSTEM.md agent roster was also missing Galen — now added. galen.md had incorrect data source paths (Obsidian) for bloodwork that lives on disk in Dropbox — also corrected.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Added Galen to SYSTEM.md agent directory. Updated galen.md and galen-bloodwork SKILL.md to point to correct Dropbox paths for bloodwork PDFs. On boot, all agents/ files should be treated as authoritative — if David asks about an agent, check agents/ first.

entries_addendum.description: When OmniFocus MCP timed out, Jarvis improvised its own AppleScript query for inbox tasks instead of deferring to the agent workflows that have correct, validated filter logic. The improvised query returned all inbox tasks including completed ones, producing an inflated and incorrect count. Jarvis then presented this number confidently in the diagnosis.
entries_addendum.correction: When MCP tools fail, fall back to osascript per SYSTEM.md — but use established agent scripts, not improvised queries. Agents (Chief) own the correct AppleScript patterns for filtered task queries. Jarvis should surface the fallback approach and let the correct agent execute it, not DIY the query.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Do not write ad-hoc AppleScript task queries. Reference agent-owned scripts or escalate to Chief for OmniFocus fallback queries. If no established script exists, note the gap rather than improvising.

entries_addendum.description: Used em-dashes repeatedly in output after being explicitly told three times not to. David flagged this as a persistent, recurring failure requiring permanent correction.
entries_addendum.correction: Never use em-dashes. Use colons, semicolons, commas, or rewrite the sentence instead.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Em-dashes are permanently banned from all output. No exceptions. Recurrence count: 3+.

entries_addendum.description: Drafted Dallas SKO follow-up email using Houston session accounts and team members (Intuit, Oxy, Live Nation, McLane, Mainsail; Houston sellers). Root cause: Plaud transcripts in staging were from April 2 Houston session; Jarvis did not search for the Dallas-specific recap email David had already sent before drafting.
entries_addendum.correction: Should have searched Outlook for the Dallas SKO recap email David sent to the Dallas team before drafting any follow-up. The correct accounts are Simpson Strong-Tie, AT&T, Entergy, and Rayburn Electric. The correct sellers are Derek, Rod, Diana, and Alexander.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: When drafting follow-ups to a specific event, always search email first for any recap or debrief the executive already sent — that document is the authoritative source of commitments, not the raw transcripts alone.

entries_addendum.description: On session resume after context compaction, Jarvis did not log err-20260403-001 immediately upon resuming. User had to explicitly prompt for the error log. The CLAUDE.md instruction states: 'When David corrects you — any correction, any agent — log it to error-log.json immediately in the same response.'
entries_addendum.correction: Error log write must happen in the same response as the correction is received. On session resume from a summary, any prior corrections in the summary must be logged before proceeding with any other work.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: On boot from a compacted context summary, scan the summary for any corrections flagged in prior turns and log them before executing any other action.

entries_addendum.description: Asked David to confirm speaker mappings for the Houston SKO sessions (Intuit/Oxy/Live Nation and McLane/Mainsail) despite these mappings having already been established in a prior turn of the same session. David had already confirmed: Intuit=Minu, Oxy=Stephen, Live Nation=Mark, McLane=Shalini, Mainsail=Oscar. Context was lost to compaction and not recovered before asking.
entries_addendum.correction: Speaker mappings were already known: Intuit=Minu Mohan, Oxy=Stephen Johnson, Live Nation=Mark Remington, McLane=Shalini Arora, Mainsail=Oscar Velazquez. Should have been applied directly without re-asking.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: When resuming from a compacted context summary, extract all previously confirmed data points (speaker mappings, decisions, confirmations) and apply them before asking the user to re-confirm anything.

entries_addendum.description: Jarvis attempted to run fetch_plaud.py directly via Bash instead of routing Plaud rename operations through Knox. Knox owns all Plaud operations per SYSTEM.md.
entries_addendum.correction: All Plaud speaker rename operations must be dispatched to Knox. Jarvis routes, Knox executes.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Any fetch_plaud.py invocation must be routed through Knox agent. Jarvis has no direct execution rights on Plaud operations.

entries_addendum.description: Incorrectly proposed Speaker 7 = Juan Bernal and Speaker 9 = Kristin Johnson. David corrected: Speaker 7 = Robyn Fuentes, Speaker 9 = Oscar Velazquez. Also flagged that Kristin Johnson and Juan Bernal were not in attendance at the Houston weekly meeting.
entries_addendum.correction: Speaker 7 = Robyn Fuentes. Speaker 9 = Oscar Velazquez. Kristin Johnson and Juan Bernal were not present at this meeting.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Do not assume all SKO attendees were present at subsequent internal meetings. Verify attendance independently.

entries_addendum.description: Incorrectly proposed Speaker 10 = Robyn Fuentes based on YPO knowledge inference. David corrected: Speaker 10 = Devlin Liles.
entries_addendum.correction: Speaker 10 = Devlin Liles.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Cross-reference against full Houston IES team roster, not just SKO attendees.

entries_addendum.description: Proposed Speaker 11 = Mark Remington tentatively but asked David to confirm rather than concluding directly from available evidence. David confirmed Speaker 11 = Mark Remington.
entries_addendum.correction: Speaker 11 = Mark Remington.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: When elimination logic clearly points to one remaining candidate and the evidence fits, commit to the identification rather than deferring to the user.

entries_addendum.description: Morning briefing workflow delivered successfully but state.yaml was left at status: in-progress instead of being updated to status: complete.
entries_addendum.correction: After step-04 delivers the briefing, state.yaml must be written with status: complete before the response is returned.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Added mandatory state.yaml completion write to step-04-synthesize-briefing.md — must write status: complete before returning the briefing.

entries_addendum.description: Ran the monthly credit card walkthrough directly instead of delegating to Chase. Chase owns the card optimizer workflows (monthly benefits review, site walkthrough, charge migration). Jarvis handled it inline, made navigation errors in Chrome, opened multiple tabs, and operated without Chase's skill context.
entries_addendum.correction: Any card review, card walkthrough, or card optimization task must be immediately delegated to Chase. Do not run inline.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Add explicit rule to CLAUDE.md boot sequence: card-related requests (review, walkthrough, which card, charge migration) route to Chase before any action is taken.

entries_addendum.description: Spawned Chase sub-agent but provided only a text handoff message without giving Chase the file paths, identity context, or Chrome session access he needed to actually execute. Chase came back unable to proceed — could not read identity files, could not access Chrome session.
entries_addendum.correction: When spawning a sub-agent, the spawn payload must include: (1) absolute file paths to all referenced files, (2) confirmation of what tools/connectors are available in the session, (3) any runtime state (e.g., active Chrome tab URL, account keys) needed to resume mid-task.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Create a spawn checklist: before Agent tool call, confirm agent has paths to identity files, relevant workflow files, active tool list, and any runtime state. Sub-agents do not inherit session context automatically.

entries_addendum.description: Reported Uber Cash and Uber One as 'NOT ENROLLED' on Amex Platinum. David confirmed both are enrolled and benefits have been received. Root cause: read benefit status from 'Your Benefit Highlights' section on the Amex portal, which does not accurately reflect enrollment or credit usage. The correct source is the 'Benefits Activity' section — must click 'Show All' to see accurate per-benefit status, credits used, and enrollment state.
entries_addendum.correction: Uber Cash = enrolled and active. Uber One = enrolled and active. For Amex benefit status, always navigate to Benefits Activity and click 'Show All' — never use 'Your Benefit Highlights' as the source of truth.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Amex portal rule: benefit enrollment status and credit usage must ONLY be read from the 'Benefits Activity' section after clicking 'Show All'. 'Your Benefit Highlights' is a marketing display — it is not reliable for enrollment or usage data. This applies to all Amex card walkthroughs.

entries_addendum.description: Card walkthrough used incorrect portal URLs for Citi, Discover, and Chase Sapphire. Generic/guessed URLs were used instead of verified login portal URLs, resulting in wrong pages loading.
entries_addendum.correction: Correct portal URLs: Amex=https://global.americanexpress.com/overview | Citi=https://www.citi.com/login | Discover=https://www.discover.com/login/ | Chase=https://secure.chase.com/web/auth/dashboard#/dashboard/overview. These are now stored in card-registry.json under portal_url for each card.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Added portal_url field to every card in card-registry.json. Chase must read portal_url from card-registry.json before navigating to any issuer portal — never guess or hard-code URLs.

entries_addendum.description: Accidentally added Starlink offer to Chase Sapphire during Chrome automation. Intended to click Dropbox tile but the Starlink 'Add offer' button was rendered in the DOM as a featured offer and intercepted the click event. David explicitly confirmed he is not a Starlink customer and said not to recommend it.
entries_addendum.correction: Scroll target tile fully into view and verify the modal that opens matches the intended merchant before clicking Add offer. Check modal title/merchant name before confirming add.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Before clicking 'Add offer' in any modal, read the modal content first to confirm the merchant name matches the intended target. Never click Add offer without verifying the modal is for the correct offer.

entries_addendum.description: Chase used mcp__Control_Chrome__open_url to navigate to card portals instead of clicking existing links on the page. David explicitly established in the previous session that navigation must happen within the current tab by clicking links — open_url should not be used when the page is already loaded and navigable.
entries_addendum.correction: When already on an authenticated portal page, navigate by clicking links (execute_javascript to click, or use page content to find hrefs and follow them). open_url is for jumping to a new issuer domain, not for in-portal navigation.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Add Rule 4 to workflows/card-walkthrough/workflow.md: In-portal navigation uses link-clicking (execute_javascript or page inspection), not open_url. open_url is reserved for initial navigation to a new card issuer domain only.

entries_addendum.description: Used window.location.href (open_url equivalent) for in-portal navigation on Citi instead of clicking nav links. David explicitly corrected: navigate with clicks, not URLs.
entries_addendum.correction: When already authenticated on a portal, navigate exclusively by clicking links and buttons via execute_javascript. open_url and window.location.href are reserved for the initial jump to a new issuer domain.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Rule 4 in card-walkthrough/workflow.md already exists but was not followed. Reinforce: zero exceptions — no URL navigation once authenticated.

entries_addendum.description: Made two protocol violations: (1) Applied skill/agent definition updates directly instead of delegating to Rigby, who owns system evolution. (2) Edited the task portfolio inline in chase.md instead of the correct skill file location. System changes must go through Rigby.
entries_addendum.correction: Any edits to agent definitions, skill files, or system configuration must be delegated to Rigby. Jarvis does not self-modify the system directly.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Rule reinforcement: Jarvis routes all agent/skill/system file changes to Rigby via Agent spawn. No exceptions, even for small one-line fixes.

entries_addendum.description: Chase's co-sell-pipeline skill commentary incorrectly attributed partner pipeline responsibility to Nick Larson (Confluent) and John Yurewicz (Microsoft), suggesting they needed to 'activate' or have pipeline conversations. David is the owner of partner relationships and co-sell pipeline — not the partner contacts.
entries_addendum.correction: Nick Larson and John Yurewicz are partner contacts, not pipeline owners. David owns the co-sell pipeline and is responsible for driving it. Commentary should surface gaps to David as the accountable party, not suggest the contacts need to move.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Update co-sell-pipeline skill to remove partner contact callouts from commentary. Commentary should address David directly as the pipeline owner. Also update Key Relationships section to reflect contacts as relationship contacts, not responsible parties.

entries_addendum.description: pipeline-snapshot skill included Past Due Opps as a data pull and reported all-Improving pipeline totals. David does not care about past due opps (operational detail, Don handles it) and only cares about One Texas (Dallas + South Texas), not all of Improving.
entries_addendum.correction: pipeline-snapshot scope is One Texas only (Dallas TX + South Texas). Always filter before reading. Remove Past Due Opps entirely — that is Don's operational layer, not David's funnel-filling view.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Rewrote pipeline-snapshot skill: removed Past Due Opps step, added explicit One Texas only scope note, changed output format to always show Dallas + South Texas + combined One Texas column. Never show unfiltered all-Improving numbers.

entries_addendum.description: During boot, Jarvis attempted to scan the Jarvis inbox via Apple Mail AppleScript. The Jarvis folder lives in Microsoft Outlook / M365, not Apple Mail. Skill doc (skills/jarvis-inbox/SKILL.md) references Apple Mail but David's Improving account is M365-based.
entries_addendum.correction: Jarvis inbox is in the M365 Outlook account, not Apple Mail. Use M365 MCP connector to access the Jarvis folder.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Update jarvis-inbox SKILL.md to reference M365 MCP (outlook_email_search or folder read) instead of Apple Mail AppleScript. Use M365 on all subsequent boot scans.

entries_addendum.description: Briefing flagged Specialty Capital fixed-bid proposal to Jerry Pender as overdue and unsent. David confirmed the email was already sent and visible in M365 sent items. Jarvis should have verified sent status via M365 before flagging.
entries_addendum.correction: Specialty Capital proposal was sent. Should have checked M365 sent items before surfacing as an open action item.
entries_addendum.systemic_fix: Before surfacing a task as overdue/unsent, cross-check M365 sent items when the task involves sending an email or proposal. Do not trust OmniFocus task open status alone for communication-type tasks.

description: Created working memory file in Obsidian vault instead of on disk only

context.action: Create file for Alice EA log

correction_taken: Deleted Obsidian file, created correct disk-based file in /people/ directory
rule_violated: Working memory files must be stored on disk only, never in Obsidian vault
user_instruction: You created that in Obsidian - never do that again. Working memory is on disk only.

description: Recommended adding 10 offers to Amex Platinum card based on historical spend patterns and assumptions, without first pulling actual YNAB transaction data for the last 90 days. Made vendor matches without validating against real recent spending history. Also opened new Chrome tabs despite explicit instruction to navigate only in current tab.
correction: Must pull YNAB transaction history (last 90 days) FIRST to build vendor-frequency map, THEN filter the 100 available offers against actual spend patterns. Only recommend offers where vendor appears in YNAB data or high-probability category spend exists. Do not open new tabs — navigate only in current tab.
systemic_fix: Update chase-card-offers-amex skill to mandate YNAB pull (Step 5) BEFORE any offer recommendations. Add explicit instruction: 'Pull YNAB data before analyzing portal data.' Also reinforce tab management: 'Navigate only in current tab, do not open new tabs.'

description: Asked the user 'Are you still in the Amex Platinum offers page in the current tab?' instead of using mcp__Control_Chrome__get_current_tab to check the tab state directly. Repeated this pattern of asking the user questions about tab state when tools exist to check automatically.
correction: Use get_current_tab() to check browser state before asking clarifying questions about which page the user is on. Do not ask the user about tab/page state — read it directly from the browser.
systemic_fix: Rule: Never ask the user 'Are you on X page?' or 'Is tab Y open?' when you have direct browser state tools available. Always call get_current_tab() first to verify state.
